title: UNESCO's Memory of the World Asia-Pacific Regional Register
focus: Inclusion of Indian literary works and the establishment of MOWCAP.
rationale: This section covers a specific recent update regarding India's cultural heritage recognition by UNESCO, highly relevant for UPSC Prelims.
sectionExcerpt: In 2024, Ramcharitmanas, Panchatantra, and Sahrydayaloka-Locana were included in UNESCO's Memory of the World Asia-Pacific Regional Register. These were authored by Goswami Tulsidas, Pt. Vishnu Sharma, and Acharya Anandvardhan, respectively. MOWCAP was established in 1998.
evidenceBullets: Ramcharitmanas, Panchatantra, and Sahrydayaloka-Locana included in UNESCO's Memory of the World Asia-Pacific Regional Register in 2024.
Panchatantra authored by Pt. Vishnu Sharma.
Memory of the World Committee for Asia and the Pacific (MOWCAP) established in 1998.

title: UNESCO/Jikji Memory of the World Prize
focus: Origin, purpose, and operational details of the Jikji Prize.
rationale: The Jikji Prize represents a distinct international award related to documentary heritage, with specific historical context (oldest movable metal print) and funding details (Republic of Korea) that are testable.
sectionExcerpt: The UNESCO/Jikji Memory of the World Prize, established in 2004, honours the world's oldest extant book printed with movable metal type, created in 1377 in Korea. It awards US$ 30,000 biennially to individuals or institutions preserving documentary heritage, funded by the Republic of Korea.
evidenceBullets: Named after the oldest extant book printed with movable metal type, created in 1377 in Korea (Buljo jikji simche yojeol).
Established in April 2004 to reward efforts in preserving documentary heritage.
Consists of a biennial award of US$ 30,000.
Funded entirely by the Republic of Korea.
Open to governments of Member States and international NGOs maintaining formal relations with UNESCO.
